etymology.description: 木(木) (mù, "tree") with horizontal stroke at root -> emphasizes the root of the tree -> "root"
etymology.interpretation: The character 本(běn, "root") is formed by taking 木(木) (mù, "tree") and highlighting its base with an extra horizontal stroke, symbolizing the root. This visualization directly leads to the meaning 'root,' as the base of a tree is its foundation and source of growth.

examples.chinese: 这本书很有趣(這本書很有趣)。
树的本质是坚固的(樹的本質是堅固的)。
examples.pinyin: Shù de běnzhì shì jiāngù de.
examples.english: The essence of the tree is strong.

examples.chinese: 从本地出发(從本地出發)，我们很快就能到达那儿(我們很快就能到達那兒)。
examples.pinyin: Cóng běndì chūfā, wǒmen hěn kuài jiù néng dàodá nà'er.
examples.english: Starting from the local area, we can reach there quickly.
